calendar.matters:
Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jul-14-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BENITO TAYLOR'S Motion To Dismiss is Off calendar. Moving party did not provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers in violation of SF Local Rule 2.6B. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/PJB)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALISA KIM, ESQ., 415.826.9160, FOR PLAINTIFF EQUABLE ASCENT FINANCIAL, LLC.
BENITO TAYLOR, 415.489.8007, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.

RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT EQUABLE ASCENT FINANCIAL, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS Sustained WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Dec-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BENITO TAYLOR, 415.489.8007, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER; No other appearances.

RULING: MR. TAYLOR PROVIDES PROOF OF SERVICE TO THE COURT AS REQUESTED. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT BENITO TAYLOR'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Continued To Dec-12-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BENITO TAYLOR's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is continued to December 12, 2013, to give the moving party the chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall provide a courtesy copy of its papers immediately with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-04-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - BENITO TAYLOR, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BENITO TAYLOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant is entitled to relief under Cal. Civ. Proc. 473.5 because he lacked actual notice of the action in time to defend. Defendant may file his proposed answer within 10 days. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/PJB

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-04-2013 Continued To Oct-04-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BENITO TAYLOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT is continued to October 4, 2013 to give Mr. Taylor the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Taylor must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 4, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mjm
